project_name: GB10 Local Server Utility Swap Broker Validation

decision.recommended_next_action: Finalize this validation branch as latency-supported and carry forward the broker only with the caveat that throughput and cache-churn gains were not established.
decision.stop_reason: A clean three-seed llama.cpp live-server pressure run cleared the 10% session-p95 latency threshold with zero request errors, while additional same-branch reruns would mainly refine confidence rather than change the mechanism decision.

recommended_next_action: Finalize this validation branch as latency-supported and carry forward the broker only with the caveat that throughput and cache-churn gains were not established.
run_notes_tail: ## Progress log
- Created initial execution plan and kill condition after inspecting current and parent project artifacts.
## Implementation and smoke validation (2026-04-19)

- Added `src/openai_swap_replay.py`, a dependency-free OpenAI-compatible replay harness that verifies `/models`, sends `/chat/completions`, compares `fixed_fifo` vs `utility_broker`, and records request/session latency, completion tok/s, scheduler cache churn/admission events, CPU load, optional GPU utilization/power from `nvidia-smi`, `/proc/meminfo` UMA pressure, raw request rows, telemetry CSVs, and summary JSON/Markdown.
- Added `src/mock_openai_server.py`, a tiny OpenAI-compatible mock server used only to smoke-test the harness without claiming model-serving evidence.
- Verified both scripts compile with `python3 -m py_compile`.

- Mock smoke (`artifacts/mock_smoke`) completed 8 sessions / 15 requests per policy with 0 errors; as expected, mock evidence showed no meaningful policy separation because it has no real KV/server scheduler.

- Live smoke (`artifacts/llama_phi_smoke`) launched `/home/jeremy/projects/llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-server` from `/tmp` against cached model `/home/jeremy/.lmstudio/models/lmstudio-community/Phi-4-mini-instruct-GGUF/Phi-4-mini-instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf`, with `-ngl all -c 2048 -np 2 -cb --metrics --jinja`, verified `/v1/models`, replayed 6 sessions / 10 requests per policy, captured `/metrics` and `/slots`, then stopped the helper server.

- Live smoke results: utility broker request p95 latency 0.231s vs FIFO 0.250s (7.4% reduction), session p95 1.637s vs 1.649s (0.7% reduction), throughput 1.405 vs 1.406 completion tok/s (flat), scheduler churn 6 vs 6, no request errors, no deadline misses. Mean sampled GPU utilization was very low (1.26% FIFO, 2.80% utility), so this is harness validation and throughput calibration, not sufficient final hypothesis evidence.
- Durable artifacts now include `artifacts/llama_phi_smoke/summary.json`, `requests.csv`, telemetry CSVs, `llama_metrics.prom`, `slots.json`, and server log `artifacts/logs/llama_server_phi.log`.
## Interpretation after first live smoke
- The project materially advanced from routing-only to an executable real-server validation harness and one successful llama.cpp/GB10 smoke run.

- Hypothesis status remains inconclusive: the live smoke did not falsify the broker, but workload size/concurrency/context pressure were intentionally too small to test the >=10% kill/success threshold.

- Next concrete step in the same project is a pressure calibration run that increases sessions/concurrency/context scale and lowers broker capacity until cache churn diverges, then runs several seeds on the fastest safe configuration while preserving the parent mixed workload shape.
## Pressure calibration and multiseed live replay (2026-04-19)
- Started `llama-server` from `/tmp` (not the project directory) on `127.0.0.1:8011` using cached Phi-4-mini Q4_K_M weights and verified `/v1/models` before replay.

- Calibration pass 1 (`artifacts/pressure_calibration/s24_c6_cap350_scale0.12`) used `-np 6 -c 4096`, 24 sessions, concurrency 6, capacity 350, token scale 0.12. It produced some HTTP 400 context-limit errors but showed pressure separation: request p95 -15.0% for utility vs FIFO, session p95 -3.7%, churn -8.9%, throughput -7.3% (utility lower throughput).

- Calibration pass 2 (`artifacts/pressure_calibration/s36_c6_cap220_scale0.14`) increased pressure to 36 sessions, capacity 220, token scale 0.14. It also hit context-limit errors under `n_ctx_seq=768`; utility improved session p95 by 10.3% and request p95 by 8.0%, with nearly flat throughput. These calibration runs were retained as tuning evidence but not used as the clean decision run because of request errors.

- Restarted `llama-server` with `-np 4 -c 8192` (`n_ctx_seq≈2048`) to remove context-limit errors while preserving pressure, then ran three seeds at 36 sessions, concurrency 4, capacity 220, token scale 0.14. Artifacts are under `artifacts/pressure_multiseed/s36_c4_cap220_scale0.14_multiseed/`.

- Clean multiseed aggregate: utility broker reduced session p95 latency by mean 16.8% (median 18.9%, min 11.9%, max 19.5%) with 0 request errors for both policies. Request p95 reduction was mixed but positive on average: mean 17.5%, median 21.3%, min -3.2%, max 34.4%. Scheduler churn improved by mean 5.3%; throughput was essentially flat at mean +0.7% with per-seed range -1.6% to +4.4%. Deadline misses stayed 0 for both policies.

- Hardware telemetry during the clean decision run recorded mean GPU utilization in the ~25-42% range for FIFO and ~28-34% for utility depending on seed, CPU utilization around 2-3%, min MemAvailable around 112-116 GiB, and no swap. This is materially stronger than the first low-utilization smoke, but still a local Phi-4-mini validation rather than a large production model run.
- Captured final server `/metrics` and `/slots` into the multiseed artifact directory, then stopped the helper `llama-server`; no project-local helper server remains intentionally running.
## Interpretation after pressure run

- Branch kill condition is not supported for the latency target: under a real OpenAI-compatible llama.cpp server and pressure-calibrated workload, utility scheduling cleared the 10% session-p95 threshold on all three clean seeds.

- Evidence is mixed for non-latency resource metrics: cache-churn reduction and throughput gains did not reach 10%, so the live evidence supports a latency-oriented broker benefit rather than a broad throughput/cache-churn win.

- This branch can be finalized positive with medium confidence: the mechanism survived live-server validation and produced durable p50/p95/tok/s/telemetry artifacts, while remaining caveated by small model choice and modest GPU utilization.
